rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LP VS. VIRGINIA WASHINGTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 6, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT VIRGINIA WASHINGTON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR, dismissal on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ZEUS LIVING, INC. VS. ENAS BIRAWI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 6, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF ZEUS LIVING, INC. Plaintiff'S Motion To Amend Judgment is DENIED. Under the circumstances of this case neither CCP 473(a)(1) nor (d) are applicable to the requested relief of amending the judgment entered per October 12, 2022 ex-parte application by inclusion of "all other occupants." At the October 12, 2022 ex-parte hearing the only issue before the Court was breach of the stipulation by Enas Birawi and entry of judgment thereon against Enas Birawi. (See Notice of Ex-Parte 1:26 and Relief Requested 5:13.) As the breach of stipulation, which provided for ex-parte enforcement, was the only issue before the Court, it was necessarily the only issue decided. The merits of the Pre-Judgment Claim of Right to Possession, its timeliness or applicability of the judgment to "all other occupants" was not properly before the Court at the ex-parte hearing for entry of judgment against Enas Birawi. Therefore, the suggested amendment of the judgment by inclusion of the words "and all other occupants," would not be subject to an amendment under CCP 473(a)(1) or (d) (e.g. mistake, clerical error, etc.). Issues pertaining to the Claim or Right to Possession and Answer by Abuzahra were not properly before the Court at the ex-parte application as such issues have not been properly noticed. Additionally, had the issues of the Claim or Right to Possession and Answer by Abuzahra been properly noticed for the ex-parte application and had the words "and all other occupants" been included in the form of Judgment presented for execution at the ex-parte hearing, the ex-parte application for entry of judgment against Birawi and all other occupants including Abuzahra would have been denied. Plaintiff would have been instructed to file a regularly noticed motion to address the Pre-Judgment Claim, Answer, and the propriety of applicability of the judgment to "all other occupants" given that unchallenged Pre-Judgment Claim and Answer were on file at the time of the ex-parte hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YERNA BUENA COMMONS ASSOCIATES, INC. VS. JUAN CARLOS SANTILLI
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 6, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT JUAN CARLOS SANTILLI APPLICATION TO PREVENT FORFEITURE DUE TO COVID-19 RENTAL DEBT Hearing Required to address the unit number. Complaint seeks possession of unit #216; rental assistance is for unit #200. Additionally, rental assistance "draw order" indicates that funds for October 2021 to March 2022 were disbursed on June 14, 2022, 6 weeks before this complaint was filed. Plaintiff to address whether payment has been received and when. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JAMES KADLECK VS. KEELEY BROOKS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 6, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT KEELEY BROOKS DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to expressly assert his interest in the property that provide him with standing. The definition of the landlord referred to in opposition includes at least 12 different possibilities, e.g. owner, owner's agent, owner's representation, owner's successor, etc. Plaintiff shall further expressly address the changed allegation form "owner" to "landlord," (which based on opposition does not include "owner" in this particular case) both of which are made in a verified pleading. Plaintiff shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
